premise: For 30 years I paid the mortgage and upkeep on my mother’s $280,000 beach cottage while my brother lived rent‑free in the city; after she passed, he produced a will — witnessed by his golf buddies — leaving the cottage to him alone and claimed I’d been 'renting' it all along. I found a cache of my mother’s letters begging me to fix this, proved the will was a forgery through a handwriting expert, won the cottage back, and now list it on a vacation‑rental site I co‑founded, where he pays me $300 a night to rent the very cottage he tried to steal.
